response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with a strong web and backend background, but his profile has no overlap with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect focused on AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design at the silicon level. His 'performance' work is entirely in software (low-latency HTTP services, not hardware), and his AI involvement is at the agent/tooling application layer rather than hardware optimization. The role requires principal-level depth in hardware systems that is entirely absent from this resume.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-conscious software engineering in low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby)
Breadth of systems experience including embedded, C/C++, and low-level tooling (NI LabWindows, robotics at Altom)
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, showing awareness of the AI domain
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or any hardware-level performance analysis
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization at the hardware or workload level
All performance engineering is in the software/network layer; no hardware-software co-design or silicon-level work is present

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No GPU/AI accelerator or DNN workload expertise
